author: uddhava dāsa
verses: jhulata śyāma, gaurī vāma, ānanda raṅge mātiyā iṣata hasita rabhasa keli jhulāyata kata sakhinī meli gāota kata bhātiyā hema maṇi jutavara hiṇḍora, racita kusuma gandhe bhora paḍata bhramara pātiyā navīna latāya jaḍita dāla, vṛndāvipina obhita bhāla cā̐da ujora rātiyā nava ghana tanu dolye śyām, rāi saṅge jhulata vāma taḍita jaḍita kātiyā tārāmaṇi juta candra hāra, jhulite duliche gale dohāra hilana duhuka gātiyā dhidhi kaṭi dhiyā tāthaiyā bola, bāje mṛdaṅga mohana rola titinā titinā tātiyā bheda paḍala grāma pura, dhīra śabada jitasura varaṇa nāhika jātiyā maṇi ābharaṇa kiṅkinī baṅka, jhulane bājaye jhunura jhaṅka jhana jhana jhana jhātiyā rādhāmohana caraṇe āśa, kevala bharasā uddhava dāsa racita pūrita chātiyā
en_translation:
Lord Kṛṣṇa is swinging, with Rādhā on His left, reveling in joyful play. With slight smiles, many gopī friends gathered together are making Them swing in amorous play while singing various melodies.

The excellent golden swing, adorned with jewels, is filled with the intoxicating fragrance of flowers. Rows of bees are alighting upon it. The branches are entwined with fresh creepers, and the beautiful forest of Vṛndāvana shines splendidly. It is a bright moonlit night.

Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose body is like a fresh rain cloud, swings with Rādhā on His left. Their beauty is entwined like lightning and a cloud. Moon-like necklaces adorned with star-like jewels sway on Their necks as They swing. Their bodies are gracefully entwined.

To the rhythmic syllables of the drum, the mṛdaṅga plays an enchanting sound. The gentle sound, conquering all other sounds, spreads throughout the villages and towns—it is beyond description.

The jeweled ornaments and ankle bells make a jingling sound as They swing. Rādhāmohana expresses hope at Their feet, and Uddhava Dāsa has complete reliance upon Them. His heart is filled with this composition.
